    Home › Aircraft › McDonnell Douglas DC-10

    McDonnell Douglas DC-10

    McDonnell Douglas · Trijet · Retired (special missions)

    The definitive American trijet.

    Length55.5 m
    Wingspan50.4 m
    Range10,620 km
    MTOW259 t
    Typical seats270
    Built446
    Our numbers

    Derived metrics

    Computed by aircraft.fyi from the core specification — see how we calculate these.

    Seats per metre#10 of 30
    4.86 seats/m typical seats ÷ fuselage length
    Range per tonne#23 of 40
    40.9 km/t maximum range ÷ MTOW in tonnes
    Span-to-length ratio#19 of 43
    0.91 wingspan ÷ fuselage length
    Sense of scale

    How big is it, really?

    The McDonnell Douglas DC-10's wingspan, against things you already know the size of.

    McDonnell Douglas DC-1050.4 m
    Football pitch (width)68 m
    Blue whale25 m
    New Routemaster bus11.2 m
    You (probably)1.75 m
    #24 of 43 by length → #15 by weight → #22 by speed →
    Specification

    The numbers

    Length (DC-10-30)55.50 m
    Wingspan50.40 m
    Height17.70 m
    MTOW (-30)259,450 kg
    Range (-30)10,620 km
    Typical seats≈270
    Engines3 × GE CF6-50
    First flight29 August 1970
    Built446 (incl. 60 KC-10)

    Compare it

    Put the DC-10 next to something

    vs Lockheed L-1011 →vs MD-11 →
    vs MD-11 vs Lockheed L-1011 Compare with anything →
    ← PreviousLockheed L-1011 TriStar Next →McDonnell Douglas MD-11

    Last verified: 2026-07-14 · Spot an error? business@luck.fyi

    The story

    What makes it different

    The DC-10 was born from the same 1966 airline requirement as the L-1011 and beat it by being cheaper and earlier. Then came the 1970s: cargo-door failures and the 1979 Chicago crash gave it the worst reputation in aviation. The design flaws were fixed, and the aircraft quietly became what the numbers always said it was — a rugged, useful widebody that sold 446 including sixty KC-10 tankers for the USAF.

    It outlived its critics by decades. FedEx flew modernised MD-10s into the 2020s; Biman operated the last passenger flight in 2014; the KC-10 Extender refuelled American wars until its 2024 retirement.

    Its final acts are its best: 10 Tanker's DC-10s drop 35,000 litres of retardant on wildfires in a single pass, and Orbis's MD-10 is a flying eye hospital with an operating theatre in the fuselage.

    Variants

    The McDonnell Douglas DC-10 line

    DC-10-10 / -30 / -40
    The passenger family — the -30 was the long-hauler.
    KC-10 Extender
    The USAF tanker — retired September 2024.
    MD-10
    FedEx's two-crew glass-cockpit conversion.
    Operators

    Who flies the McDonnell Douglas DC-10

    All 2 current operators, verified 2026-07-14. Fleet counts are approximate where marked ≈.

    US10 Tanker Air CarrierFirefighting air tankers — 35,000-litre retardant drops.4
    USOrbis InternationalThe Flying Eye Hospital — an operating theatre in an MD-10.1

    About the data. Every specification is compiled from manufacturer documents and cited per row. Operator fleet counts are approximate snapshots, marked ≈ and dated; individually verified figures carry a ✓. All silhouettes are original works of aircraft.fyi, drawn to true scale. Corrections and enquiries: business@luck.fyi — we fix errors visibly, not silently.
